meta.objective: Fix the disjointed transition from the LLM introduction to Lesson 02 by explaining why tensors, shapes, and matrix multiplication are useful for LLMs, then audit and repair equally disjointed section transitions.

tasks.title: Repair LLM curriculum continuity
tasks.objective: Make Lesson 02 explain why tensors, shapes, and matrix multiplication are the numerical language of LLMs, then repair any similarly abrupt LLM section transitions found by a complete boundary audit.
acceptanceCriteria: Lesson 02 begins from the prompt-to-response model established in Lesson 01 and explains what tensors, named axes, and learned matrix multiplication do inside an LLM before relying on notation.
Lesson 02 includes an LLM-specific worked trace, future-use map, misconception boundary, objective check, local assessment, and retry path that agree with its exact visible outcomes.
Every Foundations lesson transition and every LLM track boundary is audited for prerequisite activation, motivation, and next-use continuity; any comparable high-severity discontinuity is repaired.
Stable lesson IDs, order, routes, persistence keys, and the Orient → Learn → Try → Test → Extend contract are preserved.
Focused curriculum, objective-coverage, reader-snapshot, and lesson-specific regression tests pass.
tasks.details: Inspect the complete LLM sequence with special attention to Introduction → Tensors, all Foundations transitions, and the first lesson of every later track. Change only the smallest coherent lesson, coverage, assessment, interaction, and review records needed to remove genuine discontinuities.

acceptanceCheckResults.criterion: Lesson 02 starts from Lesson 01 and explains tensors/matrix multiplication inside an LLM before notation
acceptanceCheckResults.details: Opening begins with piece-by-piece response construction and maps prompt positions through hidden features and learned projections.

acceptanceCheckResults.criterion: Lesson 02 teaching, trace, assessment, and retry agree
acceptanceCheckResults.details: Exact objective, narrative, worked trace, quiz, objective check, NumPy example, lab, evidence transfer, and visual use the same [prompt, position, feature] contract.

acceptanceCheckResults.criterion: All Foundations transitions and LLM track boundaries audited
acceptanceCheckResults.details: Seven seams recorded; only Lesson 01→02 was equally severe, with four weaker handoff statements strengthened.

acceptanceCheckResults.criterion: Stable curriculum and teaching contracts preserved
acceptanceCheckResults.details: Lesson ID/order/routes/persistence unchanged; reader snapshots retain the complete learning sequence.
45 focused tests, 11 documentation checks, visual verifier, and diff hygiene passed.

commit.message: Task T001: Repair LLM curriculum continuity

tasks.title: Independently grade and verify continuity repairs
tasks.objective: Prove the repaired pages fulfill every changed objective, read as a coherent beginner-first sequence, and preserve the complete course regression contract.
acceptanceCriteria: An independent semantic grader passes every changed objective across explanation, causal mechanism, concrete trace, boundary, observable check, and specific retry route with no unresolved partials.
An independent continuity review confirms Lesson 01 → Lesson 02 and all LLM track boundaries clearly state why the next topic follows and where it will be reused.
Objective-coverage and whole-page review documentation is updated with honest dated evidence after grading passes.
npm run lint and npm test pass without including or overwriting unrelated worktree changes.
tasks.details: Run the independent grader required by AGENTS.md after authoring, remediate any partials, refresh only affected review artifacts/provenance, and execute the full repository checks.
